Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require Out-Of-State Plaintiff To File Undertaking
Off Calendar Jun-12-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. SANJEET GANJAM, Esq. and Atty. ANNIE NGUYEN, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (tel: 415-421-6500) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. JEFFREY THEODORE, Esq. of Braunhagey & Borden LLP (tel: 415-599-0210) for Defendant Braunhagey & Borden LLP.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP's Motion To Require Out-Of-State Plaintiff To File Undertaking.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

Defendant BraunHagey & Borden LLP moves for an undertaking from plaintiff LTC (BVI) Limited pursuant to CCP section 1030. Subdivision (b) of this provision provides that the motion "shall be made on the grounds that the plaintiff resides out of the state or is a foreign corporation and that there is a reasonable possibility that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in the action or special proceeding."

LTC does not contest that it is a foreign corporation. The issues on this motion are whether there is a reasonable possibility BraunHagey will obtain judgment in its favor and whether its cost estimates are reasonable.

The court has analyzed the case in its companion order today and its previous order on the motion for judgment on the pleadings. It does not repeat the analysis here. The court concludes that BraunHagey has a reasonable possibility of prevailing on the merits and winning judgment in its favor. LTC's negligence claim is novel; the court's decision finding Delaware's economic loss doctrine does not bar the negligence claim is a close one that the California appellate courts could disagree with; and the court has sustained BraunHagey's demurrer without leave to amend on the negligent misrepresentation claim. As BraunHagey points out, Delaware law's modified comparative negligence statute will preclude recovery if a jury finds LTC more than 50% at fault. Both parties' email hygiene practices were questionable here but BraunHagey has shown at least a reasonable possibility of persuading a jury that LTC was more at fault in light of LTC's failure to confirm the wire instructions before sending such a substantial wire, after receiving instructions with so many red flags. BraunHagey also presents evidence that, contrary to the SAC's allegations regarding the concealment claim, it was unaware that fraudsters had sent fake wire instructions to LTC until LTC informed it. (Harris Dec. paras. 5-10 & Ex. 6.)

The court turns to the amount of undertaking. BraunHagey seeks $764,685.06; LTC challenges the amount as unreasonable, mostly because it disagrees that certain categories of claimed costs can be awarded at all, and as speculative. The court finds that BraunHagey's estimate is supported by the Theodore declaration and is a reasonable estimate of the costs it "may" be awarded should it prevail in the case. That includes the cost of the discovery referee, which is a recoverable cost and not a sanction, as well as the cost of depositions and experts if BraunHagey serves a 998 demand. The action has been vigorously litigated so far and the court has no reason to doubt that will continue. Finally, LTC's counter-estimate of $100,000 is wholly unsupported.

LTC shall file an undertaking in the amount of $764,685.06 by no later than July 31, 2025.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Victoria Thomas, CSR# 12927 (tel: 559-623-3367 / email: vlthomascsr@gmail.com). (301/CVA)

Jane Hong CSR#11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, Court Reporter

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP'S Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Ninth Circuit Appeal Of Remand Order

Defendant's motion for stay is granted. (See CCP 128(a)(8) [inherent powers of court]; OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141 (2019) ["[T]he power to stay proceedings is incidental to the power inherent in every court to control the disposition of the causes on its docket with economy of time and effort for itself, for counsel, and for litigants."].) A stay is warranted while the federal court resolves the remand issue. Either party may move to lift the stay once the remand issue is finally adjudicated.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC3.1312.
